@graph.name: Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 200Tdi engine typically costs £1,200–£2,200 supply only. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on workshop location and condition of ancillary components. Main dealer new engines are no longer available — specialist reconditioning is the route.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — when maintained. The 200Tdi is considered one of Land Rover’s most durable diesels from the classic era. Its mechanical injection and simple design make it reliable, but it is now 30+ years old. Head gasket and cooling system issues are the main pitfalls; a well‑serviced example can exceed 200,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi?

acceptedAnswer.text: Head gasket failure (linked to cooling system neglect), injection pump seal wear (hard starting), and cooling system leaks (thermostat housing, water pump). Timing belt replacement every 60,000 miles or 5 years is critical — it’s an interference engine.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery 200Tdi engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Discovery is worth £4,000 and a recon engine is £1,700 (42.5%), it’s a clear keeper. If the vehicle is a tired £1,500 example, the same engine exceeds 60% — get a scrap valuation first. Collectors and off‑roaders often justify the spend regardless.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is stamped on the left‑hand side of the cylinder block, just above the injection pump mounting flange. If you’re unsure, our free reg‑lookup tool can confirm if your Discovery originally had the 200Tdi.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 200Tdi head gasket, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — a head gasket replacement is a routine job on this engine. Costing £500–£900 including skimming the head if warped, it’s far cheaper than an engine swap. If caught before overheating causes cracks, the engine will be good for many more years.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the 200Tdi. See the Era Map above for era‑specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery 200Tdi engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The 200Tdi is a straightforward unit to work on, but age‑related seized bolts and rust can add time.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery 200Tdi engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Head Gasket Failure
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, white smoke from exhaust, oil contamination (mayonnaise in header tank), loss of power.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; catastrophic overheating can warp the head, but the block rarely fails.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if any signs appear.

problems.recommendation: The 200Tdi’s head gasket is the most common weak point, usually triggered by cooling system neglect (old coolant, failing water pump, blocked radiator). Replacing the gasket promptly and servicing the cooling system (thermostat, water pump, flush) will give many more years of reliable service. Head skimming is often required if the engine has overheated.

problems.title: Injection Pump Wear (Lucas/CAV DPA)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting (especially when hot), poor idle, black smoke, loss of power, erratic running.
problems.typicalMileage: 100,000–180,000 miles (age‑related degradation of seals and internal wear).
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (reconditioning the pump, new seals and calibration).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; a full pump replacement is rarely needed if reconditioning is done.
problems.recommendation: The Lucas/CAV rotary injection pump is a robust unit, but internal seals can harden and leak over decades, causing fuel pressure loss. Early symptoms are subtle — a hot‑start issue is the classic first sign. A specialist reconditioning (with new seals and recalibration) is cost‑effective and restores performance. Ensure the timing is set correctly after any pump work.

problems.title: Cooling System Leaks (Thermostat Housing & Water Pump)
problems.symptoms: Coolant puddles under vehicle, overheating, low coolant level, steam from engine bay.
problems.repairCost: £150–£300 (thermostat housing gasket or water pump replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignored leaks lead to overheating and head gasket failure.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant is visibly leaking.

problems.recommendation: The 200Tdi’s thermostat housing is prone to corrosion and gasket failure, and the water pump can weep from the weep hole. These are cheap, straightforward fixes that prevent the far more expensive head gasket job. If you’re replacing the water pump, do the timing belt at the same time (interval 60,000 miles or 5 years) — it’s a no‑brainer.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 200Tdi? Check These First
buyIf: Documented head gasket replacement within the last 50,000 miles
Cooling system recently serviced (new water pump, thermostat, coolant flush)
Full service history with regular oil changes (every 6,000 miles)
No signs of coolant contamination or overheating in the past
avoidIf: Visible oil in coolant or mayo under oil cap — indicates head gasket issue
Evidence of long‑term overheating (warped head, cracked block)
Poor service history — these engines need regular maintenance
Injection pump timing marks misaligned or tampered with

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 200Tdi is a tough old workhorse, but it is 30+ years old. A well‑maintained example can still be a joy, while a neglected one will empty your wallet. With the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold, a £2,200 reconditioned engine on a Discovery valued at £4,000 is just 55% — worth it. On a sub‑£2,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery 200Tdi engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery 200Tdi (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: Cold Start — Listen for excessive cranking or misfiring (injection pump wear)
Service History — Including timing belt changes (every 60k miles or 5 years)
Coolant Check — Look for oil in coolant or coolant in oil (head gasket)
Smoke Check — Blue smoke? (turbo or valve seals) / White smoke? (head gasket)
Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any whistling or power loss?
Oil Pressure — Check warning light; these engines need good oil pressure
Engine Warning Light — On classic models, no OBD — rely on physical symptoms
MOT History — Check for corrosion and emissions failures
